label: Hardware Failure — EOL Replacement
thinkingSteps: Retrieving device snapshot from 4 hours ago…
Analyzing usage patterns for the past 3 months…
Checking your EOL status and hardware failure history…

diagnosticRows.label: device_status = eol_eligible  (device age: 4.3 years, policy: 4 years)
total_app_crash = 18  (threshold: < 5 in 30 days)
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ High crash rate — hardware degradation signal

diagnosticRows.label: p90_boot_time = 81s · avg_boot_time = 71s  (threshold: < 30s)
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ Very slow boot — storage component degrading

diagnosticRows.label: avg_memory_utilization = 88.4%  ·  avg_processor_time = 27.1% (paradoxically low)
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ CPU idle while RAM maxed — active swap thrashing

rootCauses.label: EOL Device — Replacement Eligible
rootCauses.detail: 4.3 years old · 3 hardware failures in 90 days
rootCauses.evidence: device_status = eol_eligible confirmed in IT asset database (policy: 4 years). avg_cpu_temp = 87.4 °C and total_app_crash = 18 (past 30d) are direct hardware degradation signals. p90_boot_time of 81s indicates storage component failure. A restart will not resolve hardware-level degradation.
rootCauses.benchmark: CPU temperature is higher than 97% of HP EliteBook G5 devices in your org

rootCauses.label: CPU Overheating — Hardware Stress
rootCauses.evidence: avg_cpu_temp (87.4 °C) and p90_cpu_temp (94.1 °C) significantly exceed the safe operating threshold. Sustained overheating accelerates component degradation and directly predicts app crashes and unexpected shutdowns. This metric is not currently surfaced to ITSE — detecting it here adds proactive value.
rootCauses.benchmark: CPU temperature is higher than 97% of HP EliteBook G5 devices in your org

benchmarkSummary.interpretation: This device is running significantly hotter and crashing more often than similar HP EliteBook G5s. These are hardware degradation signals consistent with a device past its expected lifespan.

directEscalationChips.label: 🎫 Submit a SIM ticket for replacement

inperson.title: Visit IT Support Office
inperson.body:
Your device is EOL-qualified. Bring your current laptop for a same-day replacement.

  • 📍 Building 3, Floor 2 — IT Support Office
  • 🕐 Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM
  • 🪪 Bring your employee badge
  • 💾 Your data will be migrated to the new device

No appointment needed. Most EOL replacements are processed same-day.
inperson.source: Source: EOL Device Replacement Policy · IT Equipment Database

ticket.title: Submit a SIM Ticket
ticket.body:
Your device is already flagged as EOL-qualified — your request will be pre-approved automatically.

  • Submit SIM ticket: Hardware › Device Replacement →
  • ⏱️ Processing: 2–3 business days
  • 📧 Confirmation email sent automatically
  • 💾 IT team will contact you for data migration
ticket.source: Source: EOL Device Replacement Policy · SIM Ticketing System
